# Basic mcpspy test
#
# This script tests the core functionality of mcpspy, including
# its ability to capture, format, and log MCP protocol messages.

# Setup test files with sample MCP messages
-- simple_request.json --
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":"req1","method":"initialize","params":{"capabilities":{}}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":"req2","method":"tools/list","params":{}}

-- complex_message.json --
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":"complex1","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"TestTool","arguments":{"nested":{"key1":"value1","key2":42,"array":[1,2,3]}}}}

-- multiline_split.json --
{"jsonrpc":"2.0",
"id":"split1",
"method":"split_test",
"params":{
  "multiline": true
}}

# Test basic message capture
cat simple_request.json | mcpspy -v -f output1.mcp -- echo "Test command"
! stderr "error"
# Verify colored output appears in the stderr (we look for ANSI color code \033[32m or green)
stderr "\033\\[32m" # Green color code for recv messages
grep "mcp-recv" output1.mcp
stdout "Test command"

# Verify the output file contains the captured requests
grep "initialize" output1.mcp
grep "tools/list" output1.mcp
grep "req1" output1.mcp
grep "req2" output1.mcp

# Test with pretty printing enabled
cat complex_message.json | mcpspy -v -pretty -f output2.mcp -- echo "With pretty printing"
! stderr "error"
# Verify colored output for pretty-printed messages
stderr "\033\\[32m" # Green color code for recv messages
grep "mcp-recv" output2.mcp
stdout "With pretty printing"

# Verify the pretty-printed output has proper formatting
grep "\"method\": \"tools/call\"" output2.mcp
grep "\"name\": \"TestTool\"" output2.mcp
grep "\"nested\": {" output2.mcp
grep "\"array\": [" output2.mcp

# Test with JSON scanning disabled
cat simple_request.json | mcpspy -v -json=false -f output3.mcp -- echo "No JSON scanning"
! stderr "error"
# Verify colored output still works with JSON scanning disabled
stderr "\033\\[32m" # Green color code for recv messages
grep "mcp-recv" output3.mcp
! grep "\"method\": \"initialize\"" output3.mcp

# Test with split/multiline JSON
cat multiline_split.json | mcpspy -v -f output4.mcp -- echo "Multiline JSON"
! stderr "error"
# Verify colored output still works with multiline JSON
stderr "\033\\[32m" # Green color code for recv messages
grep "mcp-recv" output4.mcp
grep "split_test" output4.mcp
grep "\"multiline\": true" output4.mcp

# Test with pipe mode (log both sides of communication)
cat simple_request.json | mcpspy -v -pipe -f output5.mcp -- echo "Testing pipe mode"
! stderr "error"
# Verify colored output in pipe mode
stderr "\033\\[32m" # Green color code for recv messages
grep "mcp-recv" output5.mcp
stdout "Testing pipe mode"

# Test timestamps format
cat simple_request.json | mcpspy -v -ts-format "2006-01-02" -f output6.mcp -- echo "Custom timestamp format"
! stderr "error"
# Verify colored output still works with custom timestamp format
stderr "\033\\[32m" # Green color code for recv messages
# Verify timestamp format has YYYY-MM-DD pattern
grep -E "[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}" output6.mcp

# Test help output
mcpspy -h
stdout "mcpspy captures and logs MCP protocol messages"
stdout "Usage"
